030440.english: Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
030440.phonetic: A-le hna-quo wu-de-li-ge nv-do ni-ga-dv tsu-da-le-nv-dv tsu-ni-tlv-gi tsu-ne-hi de-gv-wa-ti-no-he-le-i, a-le a-ni-si-yv-wi-ha du-si-ta-de a-le du-nv-wa-ne-i

030441.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏩᏂᏄᎪᎨ ᎤᏂᏣᏖᎢ, ᎠᏁᎷᎲᏍᎨ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᏂᎯ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ.  ᏚᏍᎦᏤᏃ ᏚᏅᏍᏓᏕᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎮᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030441.english: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.
030441.phonetic: A-le a-ni-s-gi-na na-s-quo gv-wa-ni-nu-go-ge u-ni-tsa-te-i, a-ne-lu-hv-s-ge hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; Ni-hi Ga-lo-ne-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi.  Du-s-ga-tse-no du-nv-s-da-de-le u-ni-ne-i-s-di-yi; a-ni-ga-ta-he-ye-no na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i.

030442.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎩᏨᏅ ᎤᏂᎨᏎ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ; ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎬᏩᏲᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏗᎩᏯᏍᎨᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏍᎩᏯᏓᏅᎡᎸᎩ ᎬᏬᏎᎮᎢ.
030442.english: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
030442.phonetic: A-le hna-quo u-gi-tsv-nv u-ni-ge-se a-le i-na-ge wu-lo-se-i; u-ni-tsa-ti-no gv-wa-yo-le-i, a-le gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, a-le gv-wa-di-gi-ya-s-ge-i, tle-s-di s-gi-ya-da-nv-e-lv-gi gv-wo-se-he-i.

030443.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎠᏎ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᏐᎢ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᎦᏥᏯᎵᏥᏙᏁᏗ ᎠᎩᏃᎮᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᏯᏆᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏨᎩᏅᏒ.
030443.english: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
030443.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i; A-se na-s-quo di-so-i de-ga-du-wa-di-sv ga-tsi-ya-li-tsi-do-ne-di a-gi-no-he-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i; na-s-gi-ye-no i-ya-qua-dv-ne-di-yi tsv-gi-nv-sv.

030444.english: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.

030501.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎬᏩᏜᏓᏫᏍᏗᏕᎨ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎾᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎨᏂᏏᎳᏗ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᎦᏙᎨᎢ.
030501.english: And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,
030501.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi, u-ni-tsa-ti gv-wa-dla-wi-s-di-de-ge u-na-dv-go-di-yi ka-no-he-dv U-na-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, Ge-ni-si-la-di v-da-lu-lo-di ga-do-ge-i.

030502.cherokee: ᏥᏳᏃ ᏔᎵ ᏚᎪᎮ ᎥᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᏓᏔᎴᎢ; ᎠᏂᎦᏯᎷᎥᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᏣᎢᏛ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏅᎩᎶᏍᎨ ᎫᏂᎦᏯᎷᏗ.
030502.english: And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
030502.phonetic: Tsi-yu-no ta-li du-go-he v-da-lu-lo-di da-ta-le-i; a-ni-ga-ya-lu-v-s-gi-s-gi-ni u-na-tsa-i-dv ge-se-i, a-le da-nv-gi-lo-s-ge gu-ni-ga-ya-lu-di.

030503.cherokee: ᏌᏉᏃ ᎠᏔᎸ ᏥᏳ ᎤᏣᏁᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᎧᏙᏯᏍᏗᏱ.  ᎤᏪᏁᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏪᏲᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏥᏳᎢ ᎬᏩᏣᎢ.
030503.english: And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon's, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.
030503.phonetic: Sa-quo-no a-ta-lv tsi-yu u-tsa-ne-i na-s-gi Sa-wa-ni u-tse-li-ga ge-se-i, a-le u-ta-yo-se-le u-s-di-gi-dv i-yv-dv wu-ka-do-ya-s-di-yi.  U-we-ne-no a-le du-we-yo-ne u-ni-tsa-ti tsi-yu-i gv-wa-tsa-i.

030504.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏲᎯᏍᏔᏅ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ, ᏗᏍᏛᎬ ᏫᏅᏍᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏂᏗᏨᎦ ᏗᏥᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᏗᏥᏂᏴᏗᏱ.
030504.english: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
030504.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-yo-hi-s-ta-nv ga-wo-ni-s-gv hi-a nu-we-se-le Sa-wa-ni, di-s-dv-gv wi-nv-s-dv a-le e-la-di ni-di-tsv-ga di-tsi-ga-ya-lu-di di-tsi-ni-yv-di-yi.

030505.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏨᏓᏆᏛ ᏙᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏏ ᎥᏝᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏲᏥᏂᏴ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎯ ᏣᏁᏨ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏓᎬᏂ ᎠᎦᏯᎷᏗ.
030505.english: And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.
030505.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no u-ne-tse hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi u-li-tsv-da-qua-dv do-gi-lv-wi-s-da-si v-tla-no go-hu-s-di yo-tsi-ni-yv; a-se-no ni-hi tsa-ne-tsv i-yu-s-di da-gv-ni a-ga-ya-lu-di.

030506.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏂᏂᏴᎮ ᎠᏣᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎦᏯᎷᏗ ᏚᎾᎵᎦᎵᏎᎴᎢ
030506.english: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.
030506.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-na-dv-ne-lv u-ni-tsa-ti du-ni-ni-yv-he a-tsa-di; a-le u-ni-ga-ya-lu-di du-na-li-ga-li-se-le-i

030507.cherokee: ᏚᎾᏁᎶᏥᏁᎴᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏐᎢᏱ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏣᎢ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏂᏍᏕᎸᎲᏍᏗᏱ.  ᎤᏂᎷᏤᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏥᏳ ᏚᏂᎧᎵᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏕᎦᏃᏴᎨᎢ.
030507.english: And they beckoned unto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.
030507.phonetic: Du-na-ne-lo-tsi-ne-le-no u-na-li-go-hi so-i-yi tsi-yu-hi u-na-tsa-i nv-da-gv-wa-ni-s-de-lv-hv-s-di-yi.  U-ni-lu-tse-no, a-le i-tsu-la tsi-yu du-ni-ka-li-le-i, na-s-gi-no hna-quo de-ga-no-yv-ge-i.

030508.cherokee: ᏌᏃᏂᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏓᏅᏁ ᏥᏌ ᏗᎧᏂᎨᏂ ᎾᎥᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏍᏆᏓᏅᏏ, ᏥᏍᎦᎾᎯᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᏴ.
030508.english: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.
030508.phonetic: Sa-no-ni-no Qui-da u-go-hv u-da-nv-ne Tsi-sa di-ka-ni-ge-ni na-v-i, hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi s-qua-da-nv-si, tsi-s-ga-na-hi-yu-ye-no a-yv.

030509.cherokee: ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᏰᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏚᏂᏂᏴᎲᎢ;
030509.english: For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:
030509.phonetic: U-s-qua-ni-go-se-ye-no a-le ni-ga-dv a-ne-hi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge na-ni-v a-tsa-di du-ni-ni-yv-hv-i;

030510.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᏤᏈᏗ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏥᎨᏎ ᏌᏩᏂ.  ᏥᏌᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏍᎦᎸᎩ; ᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏘᏂᏱᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
030510.english: And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
030510.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Tsi-mi a-le Tsa-ni Tse-qui-di tsu-we-tsi na-s-gi u-na-li-go-hi tsi-ge-se Sa-wa-ni.  Tsi-sa-no hi-a nu-we-se-le Sa-wa-ni; Tle-s-di tsa-s-ga-lv-gi; go-hi i-yu-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi ti-ni-yi-s-gi ge-se-s-di.

030511.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏝᎥᏃ ᏚᏂᏔᎳᏗᎸ ᏥᏳ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏂᏲᏐᏁ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
030511.english: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.
030511.phonetic: U-wa-tla-v-no du-ni-ta-la-di-lv tsi-yu, ni-ga-dv du-ni-yo-so-ne a-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.

030512.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᏙᎲᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏂᎬ ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎡᏙᎮᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏯᏍᏚᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᏅ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏱᏍᎩᏅᎦᎸ.
030512.english: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on his face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
030512.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne i-lv-hi-tlv ga-du-hv e-do-hv-i, gv-ni-yu-quo a-s-ga-ya ni-gv a-da-ye-s-gi u-tlv-gi na-hna e-do-he-i; na-s-gi Tsi-sa-u-go-hv u-ya-s-du-se-i, a-le u-ta-yo-se-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-yu-no o-si-yu yi-tsa-ye-lv-nv ye-li-quo yi-s-gi-nv-ga-lv.

030513.cherokee: ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛᏃ ᎤᏒᏂᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎣᏏᏳ ᏥᏰᎸᎾ; ᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.  ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᎬ ᎤᏓᏅᎡᎴᎢ.
030513.english: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
030513.phonetic: U-do-ya-nv-hi-dv-no u-sv-ni-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; O-si-yu tsi-ye-lv-na; tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-se-s-di.  Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv a-da-ye-s-gi u-tlv-gv u-da-nv-e-le-i.

030514.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏃᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎮᎾᏉᏍᎩᏂ, [ᎤᏬᏎᎴᎢ,] ᏫᏯᏓᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏁᏨ ᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸᎲ ᎠᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᏫᎲᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ.
030514.english: And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
030514.phonetic: U-ne-tse-le-no gi-lo u-no-ne-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na; he-na-quo-s-gi-ni, [u-wo-se-le-i,] wi-ya-da-na-nu-go-wi-si a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi, a-le Mo-si u-ne-tsv tsa-da-nv-ga-lv-hv a-li-s-go-lv-do-di wi-hv-ga, na-s-gi u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di.

030515.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏟᏉ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏚᏰᎵᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏃᏣᎸᎢ.  ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏦᎴ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏅᏬᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᏢᎬᎢ.
030515.english: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
030515.phonetic: A-se-no u-tli-quo i-ga-i du-ye-li-se na-s-gi de-ga-no-tsa-lv-i.  U-ni-tsa-ti-no du-ni-la-wi-tso-le u-na-du-li-s-ge u-na-dv-go-di-yi a-le tsu-nv-wo-di-yi du-ni-tlv-gv-i.

030516.english: And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.

030517.cherokee: ᎢᎸᎯᏳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᏁ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏏᏏᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏅᏓᏩᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ; ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᎵᏂᎬᎬ ᏚᎾᏗᏫᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
030517.english: And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judaea, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
030517.phonetic: I-lv-hi-yu-no hi-a nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-hi-yu da-de-yo-hv-s-ge-i, na-hna a-ni-ne A-ni-qua-li-si a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-no-si-si-s-gi de-ga-du-wa-di-sv Ge-li-li nv-da-wa-ni-lo-sv-hi, a-le Tsu-di-yi, a-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi; U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-li-ni-gv-gv du-na-di-wi-s-di-s-ge-i.

030518.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏂᏃᎴ ᎠᏤᏍᏙᎩᎯ ᎦᏅᎨ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ; ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏁᏃ ᎤᏂᏴᏔᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ [ᏥᏌ] ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᏗᏱ.
030518.english: And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought means to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
030518.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yu-quo gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-s-ga-ya u-ni-no-le a-tse-s-do-gi-hi ga-nv-ge a-s-ga-ya u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi; u-na-ne-lo-ta-ne-no u-ni-yv-ta-ni-hi-s-di-yi, a-le [Tsi-sa] i-gv-yi-tlv u-ni-nv-ne-di-yi.

030519.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏠᏨᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏴᏔᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎦᏌᎾᎵ ᎤᎾᎩᎳᏫᏎᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏧᏂᎦᏛᎴᎯᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᎾᏠᎥᏔᏂᎴ ᎤᏤᏍᏙ ᎬᏩᏠᏯᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎠᏰᎵ, ᏥᏌ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᎴᎢ.
030519.english: And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling with his couch into the midst before Jesus.
030519.phonetic: U-na-tlo-tsv-no i-yu-na-dv-ne-di-yi u-ni-yv-ta-ni-hi-s-di-yi u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv i-yu-s-di, ga-sa-na-li u-na-gi-la-wi-se-i, na-hna-no tsu-ni-ga-dv-le-hi-s-ta-ne u-na-tlo-v-ta-ni-le u-tse-s-do gv-wa-tlo-ya-s-di u-na-da-tlu-gv a-ye-li, Tsi-sa i-gv-yi-tlv u-ni-nv-ne-le-i.

030520.cherokee: ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎯᏍᎦᏯ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ.
030520.english: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
030520.phonetic: U-no-hi-yu-sv-no u-do-le-ho-sv, hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Hi-s-ga-ya, tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi.

030521.cherokee: ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᎭ?  ᎦᎪ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ?  ᏝᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏩᏒ?
030521.english: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?
030521.phonetic: Di-no-we-li-s-gi-no a-le A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-da-nv-te-le hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-go hi-a na-s-gi tsa-so-tlv-i-s-di-ha?  Ga-go gv-wa-da-do-li-s-di a-s-ga-nv-tsv-i?  Tla-s-go u-ne-la-nv-hi u-wa-sv?

030522.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏓᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᎭ ᏙᏗᎶᏓᏅᏛᎢ?
030522.english: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
030522.phonetic: A-se-no Tsi-sa u-do-le-ho-sv nu-s-dv da-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, u-ne-tse hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Ga-do i-tsa-da-nv-te-ha do-di-lo-da-nv-dv-i?

030523.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎠᎯᏗᏳ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎡᏣᏙᎵᎩ ᎠᏛᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎦᏪᏍᏗᏱ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎮᏓ?
030523.english: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?
030523.phonetic: Ga-do u-s-di u-tli a-hi-di-yu ka-ne-i-s-di-yi, Tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv e-tsa-do-li-gi a-dv-di-yi; a-le hi-a i-ga-we-s-di-yi, Ta-le-hv-ga a-le he-da?

030524.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏣᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ, (ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎩ;) ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏪᏎᎭ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᏣᏤᏍᏙ ᎯᎾᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏤᏅᏒ ᎮᎾ.
030524.english: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.
030524.phonetic: A-se-no i-tsa-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi Yv-wi U-we-tsi ye-li-quo a-ni e-lo-hi gv-wa-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv o-s-ga-nv-tsv-i, (hi-a nu-we-se-le u-lv-da-lv-v-s-gi;) hi-a ni-gv-we-se-ha; Ta-le-hv-ga, tsa-tse-s-do hi-na-gi, a-le di-tse-nv-sv he-na.

030525.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏗᏛᎮ ᏓᏂᎧᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏎ ᎤᏤᏍᏙᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏍᎨ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
030525.english: And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
030525.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-di-dv-he da-ni-ka-nv-i, a-le u-ne-se u-tse-s-do-gi, a-le tsu-we-nv-sv wu-lo-se-i, ga-lv-quo-di-s-ge U-ne-la-nv-hi.

030526.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏗᏳᏃ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏓᏕᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᎢᏗᎪᏩᏛ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ.
030526.english: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.
030526.phonetic: Ni-ga-di-yu-no u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, a-le u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-le u-ka-li-tsv-hi u-na-ye-hi-s-di u-na-da-nv-da-de-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; U-s-qua-ni-go-di-yu i-di-go-wa-dv go-hi i-ga.

030527.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸ ᎤᏄᎪᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎮ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎵᏫ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᏬᎴ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎫᏴᏗᏱ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.
030527.english: And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, Follow me.
030527.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv u-nu-go-tse-i, a-le u-go-he a-de-lv a-gi-si-do-hi Li-wi tsu-do-i-dv, u-wo-le a-ye-li e-hi a-de-lv a-gu-yv-di-yi; a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, S-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga.

030528.english: And he left all, rose up, and followed him.

030529.cherokee: ᎵᏫᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏣᎦᎵᏍᏓᏴᎾᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᏂᏅᏜᏓᎡᎢ.
030529.english: And Levi made him a great feast in his own house: and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them.
030529.phonetic: Li-wi-no u-tsa-ti tsa-ga-li-s-da-yv-na-ne-di-yi u-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-le u-wa-sv ga-ne-lv-i; a-le u-ni-tsa-te a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le nv-wa-na-da-le i-tsu-la-ha a-ni-nv-dla-e-i.

030530.cherokee: ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᎾᏤᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏚᏂᏔᎳᏬᎯᏎᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏗᏔᏍᎦ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᎢ?
030530.english: But their scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
030530.phonetic: Di-no-we-li-s-gi-s-gi-ni tsu-na-tse-li a-le A-ni-qua-li-si du-ni-ta-la-wo-hi-se-le gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-do-no i-tsu-la-ha i-tsa-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga a-le i-tsa-di-ta-s-ga a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na-i?

030531.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᏴ ᏂᏑᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏂᏚᏂᏢᎬᎾ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᏱᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎰ ᏗᏓᏅᏫᏍᎩ, ᏧᏂᏢᎩᏍᎩᏂ.
030531.english: And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
030531.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-yv ni-su-we-se-le-i; Ni-du-ni-tlv-gv-na v-tla u-du-lv-di yi-nu-na-li-s-da-ne-ho di-da-nv-wi-s-gi, tsu-ni-tlv-gi-s-gi-ni.

030532.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏱᏗᏥᏯᏅᎯᎸ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ.
030532.english: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
030532.phonetic: V-tla u-na-da-nv-ti yi-di-tsi-ya-nv-hi-lv, a-ni-s-ga-na-s-gi-ni tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi du-na-da-nv-dv-i.

030533.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᏣᏂ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏯᏃᎩᏳ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎠᏅᏍᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎬᏩᏂᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏣᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎪ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏗᏔᏍᎪᎢ.
030533.english: And they said unto him, Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees; but thine eat and drink?
030533.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; ga-do-no Tsa-ni gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ya-no-gi-yu a-ma-tli a-nv-s-go-i a-le a-na-da-do-li-s-di-s-go-i, a-le na-s-quo A-ni-qua-li-si gv-wa-ni-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; ni-hi-s-gi-ni ge-tsa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-go a-le a-na-di-ta-s-go-i.

030534.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏥᎪ ᏕᎨᎦᏨᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎤᏅᏗᏱ ᏱᏂᏕᏨᏂᏏ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏙᎲ ᎤᏕᏒᏂᎸᎯ?
030534.english: And he said unto them, Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
030534.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i; Tsi-go de-ge-ga-tsv-s-di-s-gv a-ni-ga-ta-hi a-ma-tli u-nv-di-yi yi-ni-de-tsv-ni-si a-si-quo u-ne-la-di-do-hv u-de-sv-ni-lv-hi?

030535.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏗᎵᏰᎢᎶᎵ ᎢᎦ ᏓᎨᏥᏯᏅᎡᎵ ᎤᏕᏒᏂᎸᎯ, ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎠᏅᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
030535.english: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
030535.phonetic: A-se-no di-li-ye-i-lo-li i-ga da-ge-tsi-ya-nv-e-li u-de-sv-ni-lv-hi, na-hi-yu-no hna-quo a-ma-tli a-nv-s-ge-s-di.

030536.cherokee: ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ [ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ;] ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎤᏪᏘᎯ ᏱᎦᎳᏍᏢᎥᏍᎪᎢ; ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏄᏛᏁᎸ, ᎢᏤ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎦᏣᎦᎵᏍᎪ ᎤᏪᏘ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᏤ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏓᎾᏙᎵᎪ ᎤᏪᏘ.
030536.english: And he spake also a parable unto them; No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old.
030536.phonetic: Du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-no na-s-quo [hi-a nu-we-se-i;] v-tla gi-lo i-tse a-nu-wo u-we-ti-hi yi-ga-la-s-tlv-v-s-go-i; i-yu-ye-no na-s-gi yi-nu-dv-ne-lv, i-tse a-nu-wo ga-tsa-ga-li-s-go u-we-ti, a-le na-s-quo i-tse a-gv-ha-lv-dv v-tla yi-da-na-do-li-go u-we-ti.

030537.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏧᏪᏘ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏱᏓᏟᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ;  ᎢᏤᏰᏃ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏱᏙᎦᏣᎦᎸ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏯᏤᏬᎩ, ᏗᏑᏢᏛᏃ ᏱᏓᏲᎩ.
030537.english: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
030537.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di tsu-we-ti di-su-tlv-dv yi-da-tli-s-di-s-go-i;  i-tse-ye-no gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di yi-do-ga-tsa-ga-lv di-su-tlv-dv a-le ya-tse-wo-gi, di-su-tlv-dv-no yi-da-yo-gi.

030538.cherokee: ᎢᏤᏍᎩᏃ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎠᏎ ᏗᏤ ᏗᏑᏢᏛ ᏗᏟᏍᏙᏗ; ᎩᎳ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏂᏗᎬᏩᏍᏙᎢ
030538.english: But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.
030538.phonetic: I-tse-s-gi-no gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di a-se di-tse di-su-tlv-dv di-tli-s-do-di; gi-la hna-quo i-tsu-la ni-di-gv-wa-s-do-i

030539.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏪᏘ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᏚᎩᏛ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛ ᎢᏤ ᏳᏚᎵᏍᎪᎢ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎪᎢ, ᎤᏪᏘ ᎤᏟ ᏃᎯ.
030539.english: No man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.
030539.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo u-we-ti gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di u-du-gi-dv tsi-ge-so i-gv-we-nv-dv i-tse yu-du-li-s-go-i; hi-a-ye-no ni-ga-we-s-go-i, U-we-ti u-tli no-hi.

030601.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᏄᎵᏐᏃᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏠᎨᏏ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᏓᏫᏒ ᎤᎶᏎᎢ, ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᏚᏂᏍᎫᏕᏎ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏗᎨ ᎠᎾᏍᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ.
030601.english: And it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first, that he went through the corn fields; and his disciples plucked the ears of corn, and did eat, rubbing them in their hands.
030601.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne ta-li-ne u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-gv-yi-yi nu-li-so-no-i, na-s-gi tlo-ge-si u-tsa-le-s-di da-wi-sv u-lo-se-i, gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no du-ni-s-gu-de-se u-tsa-le-s-di, a-le u-ni-s-di-ge a-na-s-go-li-ye-s-ge tsu-no-ye-ni.

030602.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏂᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏛᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ.
030602.english: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days?
030602.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-qua-li-si hi-a ni-gv-wa-ni-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no ni-hi ni-tsa-dv-ne-ha na-s-gi i-gv-dv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga.

030603.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᏁᏤᎸ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏥᎪᎵᏰᎣᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᏫ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎪᏄ ᏧᏲᏏᏍᎨ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏁᎮᎢ?
030603.english: And Jesus answering them said, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;
030603.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-ne-tse-lv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tla-s-go na-s-quo hi-a na-s-gi yi-tsi-go-li-ye-o-i, na-s-gi De-wi nu-dv-ne-lv-i, a-go-nu tsu-yo-si-s-ge u-wa-sv a-le na-s-gi tsa-ne-he-i?

030604.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏴᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎩᏎ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎨ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎦᏙᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏚᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏁᎮᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᏥᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ.
030604.english: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?
030604.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsu-yv-le U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-da-ne-lv-i, a-le tsu-gi-se a-le tsu-ge ga-du a-ga-do-di, a-le na-s-quo tsi-du-ne-le na-s-gi tsa-ne-he-i; na-s-gi ni-du-yu-go-dv-na tsi-gi gi-lo u-gi-s-di-yi a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi u-nv-sv.

030605.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
030605.english: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
030605.phonetic: A-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Na-s-quo u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv Yv-wi U-we-tsi u-tse-li-ga.

030606.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ Ꮕ-ᏩᏓᎴ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏭᏴᎴ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏕᏲᏁᎢ; ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎡᏙᎮ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏩᎢᏎᎸᎯ
030606.english: And it came to pass also on another sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
030606.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-quo nu-li-s-ta-ne nv--wa-da-le u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-i, na-s-gi wu-yv-le di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi a-le du-de-yo-ne-i; na-hna-no e-do-he a-s-ga-ya a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv u-wo-ye-ni u-wa-i-se-lv-hi

030607.cherokee: ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎬᏩᏯᏫᏍᎨᎢ, ᏥᎪ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᏬᏗᏉ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᏄᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎤᏂᏲᎮᎢ.
030607.english: And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
030607.phonetic: Di-no-we-li-s-gi-no a-le A-ni-qua-li-si gv-wa-ya-wi-s-ge-i, tsi-go gv-wa-da-nv-wo-di-quo u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv i-ga a-ne-li-s-ge-i, na-s-gi go-hu-s-di gv-wa-nu-hi-s-do-di u-ni-yo-he-i.

030608.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᎦᏔᎮ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏓᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏩᎢᏎᎸᎯ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎭᎴᏂᎦ.  ᏚᎴᏅᏃ ᎤᎴᏂᎴᎢ.
030608.english: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. And he arose and stood forth.
030608.phonetic: A-se-no a-ga-ta-he nu-s-dv da-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le a-s-ga-ya u-wa-i-se-lv-hi u-wo-ye-ni; Ta-le-hv-ga, a-le a-ye-li ha-le-ni-ga.  Du-le-nv-no u-le-ni-le-i.

030609.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏑᏓᎴᎩ ᏓᏨᏯᏛᏛᏂ; ᎦᏙ ᏚᏳᎪᏗ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᎢ?  ᎣᏍᏛᏍᎪ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎤᏲᎨ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ?  ᎬᏅ ᎠᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ, ᎠᏓᎯᏍᏗᏱᎨ?
030609.english: Then said Jesus unto them, I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?
030609.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsi-sa hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; su-da-le-gi da-tsv-ya-dv-dv-ni; Ga-do du-yu-go-di u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-i?  O-s-dv-s-go di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi, u-yo-ge di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi?  Gv-nv a-s-de-lv-di-yi, a-da-hi-s-di-yi-ge?

030610.cherokee: ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏂᏙᎸᏃ ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏄᎾᏛᏅᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ; ᎭᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏛᏁᎴᎢ; ᎤᏬᏰᏂᏃ ᎤᏗᏩᏎ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏐᎢ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
030610.english: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
030610.phonetic: Du-ka-hna-ni-do-lv-no gv-wa-du-wi-dv ni-ga-dv nu-na-dv-nv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-le a-s-ga-ya; Ha-do-ya-nv-hi-dv.  Na-s-gi-no nu-dv-ne-le-i; u-wo-ye-ni-no u-di-wa-se na-s-gi-ya so-i nu-s-dv nu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

030611.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎧᎵᏤᏃ ᎤᏂᏔᎳᏬᏍᎬᎢ; ᏚᎾᎵᏃᎮᎴᏃ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᏥᏌ.
030611.english: And they were filled with madness; and communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
030611.phonetic: U-ni-ka-li-tse-no u-ni-ta-la-wo-s-gv-i; du-na-li-no-he-le-no u-nv-sv i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi Tsi-sa.

030612.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎾᎯᏳᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏓᎸ ᏭᎶᎭ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏨᏓᏇ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ.
030612.english: And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
030612.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne na-hi-yu-i, na-s-gi o-da-lv wu-lo-ha u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-se-i, a-le u-tsv-da-que U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hv-i.

030613.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏚᏑᏰᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᏚᏬᎡᎢ^
030613.english: And when it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles;
030613.phonetic: Hna-quo-no i-ga nu-li-s-ta-nv gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi wi-du-ya-nv-he-i; na-s-gi-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi ge-sv ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv du-su-ye-se-i, a-le ge-tsi-nv-si-dv du-wo-e-i^

030614.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏈᏓ ᏚᏬᎡᎢ, ᎡᏂᏗᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎤᏅᏟ.  ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ, ᏈᎵᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏙᎳᎻ,
030614.english: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
030614.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni, na-s-gi na-s-quo Qui-da du-wo-e-i, E-ni-di-no na-s-gi-quo u-nv-tli.  Tsi-mi a-le Tsa-ni, Qui-li-gi a-le Qua-do-la-mi,

030615.cherokee: ᎹᏚ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎻ, ᏥᎻ ᎡᎵᏈ ᎤᏫᏥ, ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᏏᎶᏗ ᏣᏃᏎᎰᎢ,
030615.english: Matthew and Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called Zelotes,
030615.phonetic: Ma-du a-le Da-mi, Tsi-mi E-li-qui u-wi-tsi, Sa-wa-ni-no Si-lo-di tsa-no-se-ho-i,

030616.cherokee: ᏧᏓᏏᏃ ᏥᎻ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᎶᏄᎮᏍᎩ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
030616.english: And Judas the brother of James, and Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.
030616.phonetic: Tsu-da-si-no Tsi-mi di-na-da-nv-tli, a-le Tsu-da-si I-s-ga-la-di na-s-gi a-da-lo-nu-he-s-gi tsi-nu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

030617.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏧᏂᎦᏐᎠᏒ ᎤᏬᏓᎸ ᎤᎴᏁᎢ.  ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏧᏈᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎤᎶᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᎷᏤ ᎬᏩᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᏥᏅᏬᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᏢᎬᎢ.
030617.english: And he came down with them, and stood in the plain, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people out of all Judaea and Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;
030617.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-tsu-la-ha tsu-ni-ga-so-a-sv u-wo-da-lv u-le-ne-i.  A-le u-ni-tsa-ti gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, a-le u-ni-tsu-qui-s-di yv-wi ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi a-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi, a-le Ta-ya a-le Sa-do-ni a-me-quo-hi u-lo-di nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi, na-s-gi tsu-ni-lu-tse gv-wa-dv-ga-ne-di-yi, a-le di-ge-tsi-nv-wo-di-yi du-ni-tlv-gv-i.

030618.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎦᏓᎭ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏩᎾᏕᏯᏙᏗᏕᎩ [ᎤᏂᎷᏤᎢ]; ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᏅᏩᏁᎢ.
030618.english: And they that were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed.
030618.phonetic: A-le a-ni-ga-da-ha di-da-nv-do gv-wa-na-de-ya-do-di-de-gi [u-ni-lu-tse-i]; a-le de-ge-tsi-nv-wa-ne-i.

030619.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎾᏟᏂᎬᏁᎮ ᎬᏩᏒᏂᏍᏗᏱ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎨ ᎤᏓᏅᏬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏗᏫᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎾᏂᎥᎢ.
030619.english: And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
030619.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv a-na-tli-ni-gv-ne-he gv-wa-sv-ni-s-di-yi.  Na-s-gi-ye-no ge-sv di-da-le-hv-s-ge u-da-nv-wo-di ge-sv a-le du-na-di-wi-s-di-s-ge na-ni-v-i.

030620.cherokee: ᏚᏌᎳᏓᏁᏃ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ ᏚᎧᎾᏁ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ; ᎢᏣᏤᎵᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030620.english: And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed be ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.
030620.phonetic: Du-sa-la-da-ne-no di-ga-do-li du-ka-na-ne gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, hi-a nu-we-se-i; O-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di u-yo i-tsa-dv-hna-de-gi; i-tsa-tse-li-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i.

030621.cherokee: ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏗᏥᏲᏏᏍᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᏙᏓᏦᎸᏏᏰᏃ.  ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏗᏣᏠᏱᎯ ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᏛᏥᏰᏥᏰᏃ.
030621.english: Blessed are ye that hunger now: for ye shall be filled. Blessed are ye that weep now: for ye shall laugh.
030621.phonetic: O-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di di-tsi-yo-si-s-gi go-hi ge-sv-i; do-da-tso-lv-si-ye-no.  O-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di di-tsa-tlo-yi-hi go-hi ge-sv-i; dv-tsi-ye-tsi-ye-no.

030622.cherokee: ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎨᏥᏂᏆᏘᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏣᏓᏓᎴᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎨᏥᏐᏢᏕᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎩᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏕᏣᏙᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏲ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
030622.english: Blessed are ye, when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shall reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of man's sake.
030622.phonetic: O-si-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di i-yu-no yv-wi ge-tsi-ni-qua-ti-he-s-di, a-le ge-tsa-da-da-le-di-s-ge-s-di, a-le ga-ge-tsi-so-tlv-de-s-di, a-le a-ni-s-ga-gi-yu ge-se-s-di de-tsa-do-v go-hu-s-di u-yo na-s-gi-ya-i, Yv-wi U-we-tsi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge-s-di.

030623.cherokee: ᎢᏣᎵᎮᎵᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᎵᏔᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᎵᎮᎵᎬᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏰᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎡᏣᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏰᏃ ᏧᏂᏙᏓ ᏂᏚᏅᏁᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
030623.english: Rejoice ye in that day, and leap for joy: for, behold, your reward is great in heaven: for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets.
030623.phonetic: I-tsa-li-he-li-ge-s-di na-hi-yu i-ga, a-le de-tsa-li-ta-de-ge-s-di i-tsa-li-he-li-gv-i; gv-ni-yu-quo-ye-no u-tsa-ti e-tsa-gu-yv-e-di ga-lv-la-di; na-s-gi-ya-ye-no tsu-ni-do-da ni-du-nv-ne-le a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.

030624.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᏤᎾᎢ ᏥᎩ, ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ!  ᎦᏳᎳᏰᏃ ᎢᏥᎭ ᎢᏥᎦᎵᏍᏓᏗᏍᎩ.
030624.english: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation.
030624.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni di-tse-na-i tsi-gi, u-yo i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di!  ga-yu-la-ye-no i-tsi-ha i-tsi-ga-li-s-da-di-s-gi.

030625.cherokee: ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏗᏦᎸᏒᎯ!  ᏙᏛᏥᏲᏏᏌᏂᏰᏃ.  ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎢᏥᏰᏣᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ ᎪᎯ!  ᎤᏲᏰᏃ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏔᏩᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᏠᏱᎮᏍᏗ.
030625.english: Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. Woe unto you that laugh now! for ye shall mourn and weep.
030625.phonetic: U-yo-i-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di di-tso-lv-sv-hi!  do-dv-tsi-yo-si-sa-ni-ye-no.  U-yo-i-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di i-tsi-ye-tsa-s-gi tsi-gi go-hi!  u-yo-ye-no i-tsa-da-nv-ta-wa-de-ge-s-di a-le de-tsa-tlo-yi-he-s-di.

030626.cherokee: ᎤᏲᎢᏳ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎾᎯᏳ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏴᏫ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎨᏥᏃᎮᏍᎨᏍᏗ!  ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏰᏃ ᏧᏂᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏂᏚᏂᎸᏉᏕ ᎤᎾᏠᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
030626.english: Woe unto you, when all men shall speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
030626.phonetic: U-yo-i-yu i-tsa-li-s-da-ne-di na-hi-yu ni-ga-di-yu yv-wi o-si-yu ge-tsi-no-he-s-ge-s-di!  na-s-gi-ya-ye-no tsu-ni-ga-yv-li-ge ni-du-ni-lv-quo-de u-na-tlo-na-s-di a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.

030627.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ ᏂᎯ ᏥᏍᎩᏯᏛᎦᏁᎭ; ᏕᏥᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏍᎦᎩ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᏂᏕᏣᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏂᏆᏘᎯ,
030627.english: But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
030627.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha ni-hi tsi-s-gi-ya-dv-ga-ne-ha; De-tsi-ge-yu-se-s-di ge-tsi-s-ga-gi, o-s-dv ni-de-tsa-dv-ne-he-s-di ge-tsi-ni-qua-ti-hi,

030628.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛ ᏕᏥᏁᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᎨᏥᏐᏢᏗᏍᎩ.
030628.english: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
030628.phonetic: O-s-dv de-tsi-ne-tse-he-s-di ge-tsi-s-gi-nv-di-s-gi, a-le i-tsa-da-do-li-s-di-s-ge-s-di ge-tsi-do-li-s-di-yi ga-ge-tsi-so-tlv-di-s-gi.

030629.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎯᎪᏆᎵᎢ ᎢᏨᏂᎮᏍᏗ ᏐᎢᏱ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᏯᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎤᏛᏃᎯ ᏣᎾᎡᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏣᏄᏬ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎯᎨᏳᏔᏅᎩ.
030629.english: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloke forbid not to take thy coat also.
030629.phonetic: Gi-lo-no hi-go-qua-li-i i-tsv-ni-he-s-di so-i-yi na-s-quo hi-ya-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-he-s-di; gi-lo-no u-dv-no-hi tsa-na-e-he-s-di, na-s-quo ha-wi-ni tsa-nu-wo tle-s-di hi-ge-yu-ta-nv-gi.

030630.cherokee: ᎾᏂᎥ ᎨᏣᏔᏲᏎᎯ ᏕᎯᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏃ ᏣᎾᎥ ᏣᎩᎡᎯ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎯᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ.
030630.english: Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again.
030630.phonetic: Na-ni-v ge-tsa-ta-yo-se-hi de-hi-ne-he-s-di; go-hu-s-di-no tsa-na-v tsa-gi-e-hi tle-s-di hi-ta-yo-se-lv-gi.

030631.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏃ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎢᎨᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᏕᏣᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
030631.english: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
030631.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya-no nu-s-dv i-tsa-du-li-s-gv i-ge-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi yv-wi, na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo ni-de-tsa-dv-ne-he-s-di.

030632.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎨᏥᎨᏳᎯᏉ [ᎤᏅᏒ] ᏱᏗᏥᎨᏳᎭ, ᎦᏙ ᎢᏥᎭ ᎦᏰᏣᎵᎡᎵᏤᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏚᏂᎨᏳᏐ ᎬᏩᏂᎨᏳᎯ.
030632.english: For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.
030632.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no ge-tsi-ge-yu-hi-quo [u-nv-sv] yi-di-tsi-ge-yu-ha, ga-do i-tsi-ha ga-ye-tsa-li-e-li-tse-di ge-sv-i?  A-ni-s-ga-na-ye-no na-s-quo du-ni-ge-yu-so gv-wa-ni-ge-yu-hi.

030633.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏱᏂᏗᏣᏛᏁᎭ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᎨᏣᏛᏁᎯ, ᎦᏙ ᎢᏥᎭ ᎦᏰᏣᎵᎡᎵᏤᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎰᎢ.
030633.english: And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same.
030633.phonetic: I-yu a-le o-s-dv yi-ni-di-tsa-dv-ne-ha o-s-dv i-ge-tsa-dv-ne-hi, ga-do i-tsi-ha ga-ye-tsa-li-e-li-tse-di ge-sv-i?  A-ni-s-ga-na-ye-no na-s-quo na-s-gi-ya na-na-dv-ne-ho-i.

030634.cherokee: ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᏛᎪᎩᏁᎵ ᏗᏤᎵᏎᎯ ᏱᏗᏣᏙᎳᏍᏗᎭ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏥᎭ ᎦᏰᏣᎵᎡᎵᏤᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᏓᎾᏙᎳᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎥᎨᏥᏁᏗᏱ.
030634.english: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
030634.phonetic: I-yu a-le dv-go-gi-ne-li di-tse-li-se-hi yi-di-tsa-do-la-s-di-ha; ga-do i-tsi-ha ga-ye-tsa-li-e-li-tse-di ge-sv-i?  A-ni-s-ga-na-ye-no na-s-quo a-ni-s-ga-na da-na-do-la-s-di-s-go-i, na-s-gi i-ga-i ta-li-ne v-ge-tsi-ne-di-yi.

030635.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏕᏥᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏍᎦᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏕᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᎡᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎳᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏚᎩ ᏂᏨᏒᎾ ᎥᎡᏥᏂᏗᏱ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ; ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎡᏣᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏩᏍᏛᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏳ ᎡᎯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ; ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏂᏓᏛᏁᎰ ᏄᎾᎵᎮᎵᏣᏛᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏲᎢ.
030635.english: But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.
030635.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni de-tsi-ge-yu-se-s-di ge-tsi-s-ga-gi, a-le o-s-dv de-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-e-he-s-di, a-le i-tsa-da-do-la-s-di-s-ge-s-di u-du-gi ni-tsv-sv-na v-e-tsi-ni-di-yi go-hu-s-di; u-tsa-ti-no e-tsa-gu-yv-e-di ge-se-s-di, wa-s-dv-no Ga-lv-la-di-yu e-hi tsu-we-tsi ge-se-s-di ni-hi; na-s-gi-ye-no o-s-dv ni-da-dv-ne-ho nu-na-li-he-li-tsa-dv-na a-le u-ni-yo-i.

030636.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎵᏣᏘᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏥᏙᏓ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏗᏳ ᏥᎩ.
030636.english: Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.
030636.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsa-da-do-li-tsa-ti-yu ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi-ya I-tsi-do-da na-s-quo u-da-do-li-tsa-di-yu tsi-gi.

030637.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᎳᏫᏎᎸᎩ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᏰᏥᎳᏫᏏ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏗᏣᏓᏚᎪᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏙᎦᏰᏧᎪᏓᏏ; ᏕᏥᏙᎵᎨᏍᏗ ᎨᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎯ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎠᏎ ᎡᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ;
030637.english: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:
030637.phonetic: Tle-s-di i-tsa-da-la-wi-se-lv-gi, ni-hi-no v-tla yv-ga-ye-tsi-la-wi-si; tle-s-di di-tsa-da-du-go-da-ne-lv-gi, ni-hi-no v-tla yi-do-ga-ye-tsu-go-da-si; de-tsi-do-li-ge-s-di ge-tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-hi, ni-hi-no a-se e-tsi-do-li-s-di ge-se-s-di;

030638.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᏂᎯᏃ ᎠᏎ ᎡᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᏟᎶᏛ, ᎠᏍᏓᏲᏔᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏖᎸᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏤᏬᎩ, ᎨᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ ᏕᏣᏓᏠᎲᎢ.  ᏄᏍᏛᏰᏃ ᎢᏣᏓᏟᎶᎡᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎡᏣᏟᎶᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ;
030638.english: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.
030638.phonetic: I-tsa-da-ne-he-s-di, ni-hi-no a-se e-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di; o-s-dv a-tli-lo-dv, a-s-da-yo-ta-nv-hi, a-le a-te-lv-nv-hi, a-le gv-tse-wo-gi, ge-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di yv-wi de-tsa-da-tlo-hv-i.  Nu-s-dv-ye-no i-tsa-da-tli-lo-e-lv na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo e-tsa-tli-lo-e-di ge-se-s-di;

030639.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ; ᏥᎪᏗᎨᏫ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏯᏘᏄᎦ ᏗᎨᏫ?  ᏝᏍᎪ ᎢᏧᎳ ᎠᏔᎴᏒ ᏱᏙᎬᏂᎸᎩ?
030639.english: And he spake a parable unto them, Can the blind lead the blind? shall they not both fall into the ditch?
030639.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-i; Tsi-go-di-ge-wi ye-li-quo ya-ti-nu-ga di-ge-wi?  tla-s-go i-tsu-la a-ta-le-sv yi-do-gv-ni-lv-gi?

030640.cherokee: ᏗᏕᎶᏆᏍᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᏱᎾᏥᎸᏉᏙ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏧᏪᏲᎲᏍᎩ; ᎾᏂᎥᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏛᎯ ᏧᏁᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
030640.english: The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.
030640.phonetic: Di-de-lo-qua-s-gi v-tla u-tli yi-na-tsi-lv-quo-do e-s-ga-quo tsu-we-yo-hv-s-gi; na-ni-v-s-gi-ni u-ni-s-qua-dv-hi tsu-ne-yo-hv-s-gi nu-s-dv na-s-gi-ya ge-se-s-di.

030641.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎯᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᎤᏢᏓᎸᏛ ᎤᎦᏑᎲᎢ ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᏁᎵᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᎢᎨᏐ ᎤᏃᏍᏛ ᎠᏓ ᏨᏒ ᏣᎦᏑᎲᎢ?
030641.english: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
030641.phonetic: A-le ga-do-no i-hi-go-wa-ti-s-go u-tlv-da-lv-dv u-ga-su-hv-i di-s-da-da-nv-tli, ne-li-s-gv-na-no i-ge-so u-no-s-dv a-da tsv-sv tsa-ga-su-hv-i?

030642.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏂᏪᏏ ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ; ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎬᎸᏏ ᎤᏢᏓᎸᏛ ᏣᎦᏑᎲᎢ, ᏨᏒᏃ ᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎾ ᎤᏃᏍᏛ ᎠᏓ ᏨᏒ ᏣᎦᏑᎲᎢ?  ᏣᏠᎾᏍᏗ!  ᎢᎬᏱ ᎯᎸ ᎤᏃᏍᏛ ᎠᏓ ᏨᏒ ᏣᎦᏑᎲᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏂᎨᏒᎢᏳ ᏣᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎯᎸᎡᏗᏱ ᎤᏢᏓᎸᏛ ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎤᎦᏑᎲᎢ.
030642.english: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother's eye.
030642.phonetic: A-le ga-do yi-ga-li-s-do-da hi-a yi-ni-we-si di-s-da-da-nv-tli; Di-na-da-nv-tli gv-lv-si u-tlv-da-lv-dv tsa-ga-su-hv-i, tsv-sv-no ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv-na u-no-s-dv a-da tsv-sv tsa-ga-su-hv-i?  Tsa-tlo-na-s-di!  i-gv-yi hi-lv u-no-s-dv a-da tsv-sv tsa-ga-su-hv-i, hna-quo-no gv-ni-ge-sv-i-yu tsa-go-wa-dv-di-yi ge-se-s-di hi-lv-e-di-yi u-tlv-da-lv-dv di-s-da-da-nv-tli u-ga-su-hv-i.

030643.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛᏰᏃ ᏡᎬ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲ ᏯᏓᏛᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᏡᎬ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏯᏓᏛᏍᎪᎢ.
030643.english: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
030643.phonetic: O-s-dv-ye-no tlu-gv v-tla u-yo ya-da-dv-s-go-i; a-le u-yo tlu-gv v-tla o-s-dv ya-da-dv-s-go-i.

030644.cherokee: ᏡᎬᏰᏃ  ᎪᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏐ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᏓᏛᏍᎬᎢ; ᏥᏍᏚᏂᎩᏍᏗᎯᏰᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏒᎦᏔ-ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏱᏓᎾᏕᏍᎪ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏖᎸᎳᏗ ᏱᏓᎾᏕᏍᎪᎢ.
030644.english: For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
030644.phonetic: Tlu-gv-ye-no  go-li-s-do-di ge-so nu-s-dv u-wa-sv a-da-dv-s-gv-i; tsi-s-du-ni-gi-s-di-hi-ye-no v-tla sv-ga-ta--i-yu-na-s-di yi-da-na-de-s-go yv-wi, a-le a-nu-ga-lv-hi v-tla te-lv-la-di yi-da-na-de-s-go-i.

030645.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ; ᎤᏲᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏲ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏛ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ ᎤᏲ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ; ᎤᏣᏛᏰᏃ ᎤᎾᏫ ᎤᏪᎲ ᎠᎰᎵ ᎧᏁᎪᎢ.
030645.english: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.
030645.phonetic: O-s-dv yv-wi o-s-dv u-s-qua-ni-go-dv u-na-wi-yi o-s-dv ge-sv ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i; u-yo-no yv-wi u-yo u-s-qua-ni-go-dv u-na-wi-yi u-yo ge-sv ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i; u-tsa-dv-ye-no u-na-wi u-we-hv a-ho-li ka-ne-go-i.

030646.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᏃ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎢᏍᎩᏲᏎᎰᎢ, ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎬᏃ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎲᎾ ᎢᎨᏐᎢ?
030646.english: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
030646.phonetic: A-le ga-do-no Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, i-s-gi-yo-se-ho-i, ni-tsi-we-s-gv-no ni-tsa-dv-ne-hv-na i-ge-so-i?

030647.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎠᎩᎷᏤᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏛᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᏪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᏓᏨᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎵ ᏓᏤᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ;
030647.english: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
030647.phonetic: Gi-lo a-gi-lu-tse-he-s-di, a-le a-dv-gi-s-ge-s-di ni-tsi-we-s-gv-i, a-le na-s-gi na-dv-ne-he-s-di, da-tsv-na-nu-go-wi-se-li da-tse-lv na-s-gi;

030648.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏓᏤᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏧᏁᏍᎨᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏧᏍᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎫᏍᏛᏗ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏧᏝᏁᎢ; ᎤᏃᎱᎦᏅᏃ ᎡᏉᏂ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎬᏂᎵᎯᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎬᏩᏖᎸᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ; ᏅᏲᎯᏰᏃ ᎦᎧᎮᎢ.
030648.english: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
030648.phonetic: A-s-ga-ya da-tse-lv na-s-gi na tsu-ne-s-ge-he-i, a-le ha-wi-ni tsu-s-go-se-i, a-le ga-gu-s-dv-di nv-yo-hi tsu-tla-ne-i; u-no-hu-ga-nv-no e-quo-ni na-hna a-da-ne-lv a-s-da-yi-yu gv-ni-li-hi-he-i, a-le v-tla gv-wa-te-lv-di yi-ge-se-i; nv-yo-hi-ye-no ga-ka-he-i.

030649.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎤᏛᎦᏅᎯ, ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎾᏃ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏓᏤᎸ ᏂᏚᎫᏍᏓᎥᎾ ᎦᏙᎯᏉ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᏁᏍᎨᎮᎢ; ᎡᏉᏂᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎬᏂᎵᎯᎮᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏪᏇᏴᏎᎢ; ᎤᏍᎦᏎᏗᏳᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᏲᏨ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
030649.english: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
030649.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na u-dv-ga-nv-hi, nu-dv-ne-lv-na-no, na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya da-tse-lv ni-du-gu-s-da-v-na ga-do-hi-quo a-da-ne-lv tsu-ne-s-ge-he-i; e-quo-ni-no na-hna u-li-ni-gi-di-yu gv-ni-li-hi-he-i, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-we-que-yv-se-i; u-s-ga-se-di-yu-no nu-li-s-ta-ne u-yo-tsv na-s-gi a-da-ne-lv-i.

030701.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬ ᏴᏫ, ᎨᏆᏂ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ.
030701.english: Now when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
030701.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-s-qua-dv ni-ga-dv ga-wo-ni-s-gv a-na-dv-gi-s-gv yv-wi, Ge-qua-ni wu-yv-le-i.

030702.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏗᏖᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏰᎸᎯ, ᎤᏢᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎱᏏᏕᎾ ᎨᏎᎢ.
030702.english: And a certain centurion's servant, who was dear unto him, was sick, and ready to die.
030702.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi di-te-ni-do-hi u-nv-si-da-s-di, o-s-dv u-ye-lv-hi, u-tlv-ge-i, a-le u-yo-hu-si-de-na ge-se-i.

030703.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᏚᏅᏎ ᏤᏙᎲ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᏭᏔᏲᏎᎮ ᏅᏓᏳᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏅᏬᏗᏱ ᎤᏅᏏᏗᏍᏗ.
030703.english: And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
030703.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no a-tsi-no-he-s-gv u-dv-ga-nv, du-nv-se tse-do-hv di-ni-la-wi-gi A-ni-tsu-si tsu-na-tse-li-ga, wu-ta-yo-se-he nv-da-yu-we-nv-s-di-yi u-nv-wo-di-yi u-nv-si-di-s-di.

030704.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎬᏩᏍᏗ ᏰᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏅᏘᏯᏛᏁᎵ;
030704.english: And when they came to Jesus, they besought him instantly, saying, That he was worthy for whom he should do this:
030704.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv, u-tsa-ti gv-wa-s-di ye-ta-ne-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; U-da-nv-ti-yu na-s-gi hi-a tsi-nv-ti-ya-dv-ne-li;

030705.cherokee: ᎤᎨᏳᎯᏳᏰᏃ ᎢᎩᏠᏱ ᎨᏒ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᏁᏍᎨᎸ ᏗᎩᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ.
030705.english: For he loveth our nation, and he hath built us a synagogue.
030705.phonetic: U-ge-yu-hi-yu-ye-no i-gi-tlo-yi ge-sv yv-wi, a-le i-ga-ne-s-ge-lv di-gi-la-wi-i-s-di-yi.

030706.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᎥᏉ ᎤᎷᎴ ᏗᏓᏁᎸ, ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏙᏧᏅᏎ ᏧᎵᎢ ᏂᏙᏓᎬᏩᏠᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏧᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏨᏒ ᏣᏓᏕᏯᏙᏔᏅᎩ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏰᎵ ᏱᏂᎪᎯ ᏣᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᏁᎸᎢ ᏥᎾᏆᏍᏗᏉ;
030706.english: Then Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying unto him, Lord, trouble not thyself: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof:
030706.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-s-da-wa-dv-se-i.  Hna-quo-no na-v-quo u-lu-le di-da-ne-lv, a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi di-ti-ni-do-hi do-tsu-nv-se tsu-li-i ni-do-da-gv-wa-tlo-hi-s-di-yi, hi-a nv-tsu-we-se-le-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tle-s-di tsv-sv tsa-da-de-ya-do-ta-nv-gi; v-tla-ye-no ye-li yi-ni-go-hi tsa-yv-s-di-yi tsi-ne-lv-i tsi-na-qua-s-di-quo;

030707.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏰᎵ ᏂᎪᎢ ᏱᏇᎵᏎ ᏫᎬᎷᏤᏗᏱ; ᎯᏁᎩᏉᏍᎩᏂ, ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ ᏧᏳᏗᏩᏏ.
030707.english: Wherefore neither thought I myself worthy to come unto thee: but say in a word, and my servant shall be healed.
030707.phonetic: V-tla a-le na-s-quo ye-li ni-go-i yi-que-li-se wi-gv-lu-tse-di-yi; hi-ne-gi-quo-s-gi-ni, tsi-nv-si-da-s-di-no tsu-yu-di-wa-si.

030708.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᏆᏓᏁᎶᏗᏉ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᎧᎭ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᏕᎬᏆᏁᎶᏙᎢ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎡᎪᎢ; ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏃ ᎡᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᏗᎡᎪᎢ; ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎿᎭᏛᎦ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏛᏁᎰᎢ.
030708.english: For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say unto one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
030708.phonetic: A-yv-ye-no na-s-quo di-qua-da-ne-lo-di-quo, a-le di-gi-ka-ha a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi a-le a-yv de-gv-qua-ne-lo-do-i, gi-lo-no He-na tsi-yo-si, e-go-i; nv-wa-da-le-no E-he-na tsi-yo-si, di-e-go-i; tsi-nv-si-da-s-di-no Hi-a hna-dv-ga tsi-yo-si, na-s-gi na-dv-ne-ho-i.

030709.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎵ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ; ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏯᎩᏩᏛᎲ.
030709.english: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
030709.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no na-s-gi hi-a u-dv-ga-nv, u-s-qua-ni-go-se-le-i, a-le u-ga-ta-hv-se-i, a-le hi-a ni-du-we-se-li yv-wi na-s-gi nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-dv; hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-ha; V-tla na-s-quo I-si-li a-ne-hv na-s-gi i-ga-i go-hi-yu-di ge-sv ya-gi-wa-dv-hv.

030710.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ Ꮎ ᏅᏓᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ, ᏫᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᏗᏓᏁᎸ, ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎤᏢᏨᎯ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᎮ ᎤᏗᏩᏒᎯ ᎢᎨᏎᎢ.
030710.english: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the servant whole that had been sick.
030710.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no na nv-da-ge-tsi-nv-si-dv, wi-u-ni-lu-tsv di-da-ne-lv, a-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di u-tlv-tsv-hi u-ni-wa-dv-he u-di-wa-sv-hi i-ge-se-i.

030711.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᎾᏱᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ; ᏰᎵᏃ ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ.
030711.english: And it came to pass the day after, that he went into a city called Nain; and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.
030711.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne u-gi-tsv-dv, na-s-gi di-ga-du-hv Na-yi-ni tsu-do-i-dv wu-yv-le-i; ye-li-no a-ni-go-di-yu gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-se-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi.

030712.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎪᏢᏒ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎾᎥ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎠᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᎨ ᎠᏂᏂᏎᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏎ ᎤᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ; ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏎᎢ.
030712.english: Now when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold, there was a dead man carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow: and much people of the city was with her.
030712.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi go-tlv-sv ga-du-hv na-v u-lu-tsv, gv-ni-yu-quo a-s-ga-ya u-yo-hu-sv-hi a-ni-nu-go-wi-s-ge a-ni-ni-se-i, u-wa-sv-hi-yu u-we-tsi ge-se u-tsi, a-le na-s-gi u-wo-su-lo-tsv-hi ge-se-i; u-ni-tsa-te-no yv-wi ga-du-hv a-ne-hi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-se-i.

030713.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏪᏙᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏗᏣᏠᏱᎸᎩ.
030713.english: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said unto her, Weep not.
030713.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-go-hv u-we-do-li-tse-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Tle-s-di di-tsa-tlo-yi-lv-gi.

030714.cherokee: ᎤᎷᏨᏃ ᎤᏏᏔᏕ ᎠᏥᏢᏛᎢ, ᎬᏩᏁᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᎴᎾᏫᏍᏔᏁᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᏫᏅ, ᎭᏗᏛ, ᎬᏲᏎᎭ.
030714.english: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare him stood still. And he said, Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.
030714.phonetic: U-lu-tsv-no u-si-ta-de a-tsi-tlv-dv-i, gv-wa-ne-gi-no u-na-le-na-wi-s-ta-ne-i.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Hi-wi-nv, Ha-di-dv, gv-yo-se-ha.

030715.cherokee: ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯᏃ ᎤᏗᏛᎮ ᎤᏪᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎤᏬᏂᏎᎢ; ᎤᏥᏃ ᎤᏪᎧᏁᎴᎢ.
030715.english: And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he delivered him to his mother.
030715.phonetic: U-yo-hu-sv-hi-no u-di-dv-he u-we-ne-i, a-le u-le-nv-he u-wo-ni-se-i; u-tsi-no u-we-ka-ne-le-i.

030716.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᎾᏰᏎᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᎩ ᎢᏕᎲᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏓᏩᏛᎯᎦ ᏧᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ.
030716.english: And there came a fear on all: and they glorified God, saying, That a great prophet is risen up among us; and, That God hath visited his people.
030716.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no u-ni-na-ye-se-i; a-le u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; A-tsi-lv-quo-di a-do-le-ho-s-gi ga-na-nu-go-gi i-de-hv-i; a-le hi-a; U-ne-la-nv-hi da-wa-dv-hi-ga tsu-tse-li yv-wi.

030717.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᏧᏗᏱ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏚᏰᎵᏏᏙᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030717.english: And this rumour of him went forth throughout all Judaea, and throughout all the region round about.
030717.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a a-tsi-no-he-s-gv Tsu-di-yi ni-gv-na-dv du-ye-li-si-do-le-i, a-le na-hna gv-wa-du-wi-dv ge-sv-i.

030718.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᏁᎴᎢ.
030718.english: And the disciples of John shewed him of all these things.
030718.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi na-s-gi hi-a ni-ga-dv gv-wa-no-ne-le-i.

030719.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮ ᏥᏌ ᏤᏙᎲ ᏚᏅᏎ ᎯᎠ ᏫᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏂᎯᏍᎪ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎩ?  ᏥᎪᎨ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎣᏥᎦᏕᏃᎮᏍᏗ?
030719.english: And John calling unto him two of his disciples sent them to Jesus, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
030719.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no a-ni-ta-li gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi wi-du-ya-nv-he Tsi-sa tse-do-hv du-nv-se hi-a wi-nu-we-se-i; Ni-hi-s-go u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-gi?  Tsi-go-ge nv-wa-da-le o-tsi-ga-de-no-he-s-di?

030720.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᏦᎩᏂᏅᏒ ᎢᏍᏛᎷᏤᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏗᎦᏪᎭ; ᏂᎯᏍᎪ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ?  ᏥᎪᎨ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎣᏥᎦᏖᏃᎮᏍᏗ?
030720.english: When the men were come unto him, they said, John Baptist hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?
030720.phonetic: A-ni-s-ga-ya-no gv-wa-lu-tse-lv, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi tso-gi-ni-nv-sv i-s-dv-lu-tse-di-yi, hi-a nv-di-ga-we-ha; Ni-hi-s-go u-lu-hi-s-di tsi-ge-sv-gi?  Tsi-go-ge nv-wa-da-le o-tsi-ga-te-no-he-s-di?

030721.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᏚᏅᏩᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏂᏢᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏳᎩ ᏚᏂᏱᎵᏙᎲ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏲᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏩᏂᏱᎵᏙᎲᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏄᏩᏁᎴᎢ.
030721.english: And in that same hour he cured many of their infirmities and plagues, and of evil spirits; and unto many that were blind he gave sight.
030721.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-quo-no du-nv-wa-ne u-ni-tsa-ti du-ni-tlv-gv a-le v-yu-gi du-ni-yi-li-do-hv, a-le u-ni-yo-di-da-nv-do gv-wa-ni-yi-li-do-hv-i; a-le u-ni-tsa-ti di-ni-ge-wi gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-di nu-wa-ne-le-i.

030722.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏥᏍᏕᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᏪᏍᏗᏃᎲᏏ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏗᎪᎲ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏍᏓᏛᎦᏅᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎨᏫ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎢ, ᏗᏂᏲᎤᎵ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᏓᏰᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᎨᏥᏅᎦᎵᏍᎬᎢ, ᏧᏂᎵᎡᏅ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬᎢ, ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏕᎨᎦᎴᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏲᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᎦᎵᏥᏙᏁᎲᎢ.
030722.english: Then Jesus answering said unto them, Go your way, and tell John what things ye have seen and heard; how that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, to the poor the gospel is preached.
030722.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsi-sa u-ne-tsv hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; Tsi-s-de-na a-le Tsa-ni we-s-di-no-hv-si nu-s-dv i-s-di-go-hv a-le nu-s-dv i-s-da-dv-ga-nv-i; na-s-gi di-ni-ge-wi a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv-i, di-ni-yo-u-li a-ne-do-hv-i, a-da-ye-s-gi tsu-ni-tlv-gi ge-tsi-nv-ga-li-s-gv-i, tsu-ni-li-e-nv a-na-dv-gi-s-gv-i, tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi de-ge-ga-le-di-s-gv-i, u-yo-no i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv ge-ga-li-tsi-do-ne-hv-i.

030723.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏴ ᏂᏗᏥᏲᏕᏍᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
030723.english: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.
030723.phonetic: A-le o-si-yu i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di gi-lo a-yv ni-di-tsi-yo-de-s-di-s-gv-na i-ge-se-s-di.

030724.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏣᏂ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏅᏏᏛ ᎢᎤᏁᏅ, ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏣᏂ ᏚᏁᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏴᏫ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ?  ᏩᏐᎾᏍᎪ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎤᏖᎸᎮᏍᎬᎢ?
030724.english: And when the messengers of John were departed, he began to speak unto the people concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness for to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
030724.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Tsa-ni ni-do-da-yu-nv-si-dv i-u-ne-nv, u-le-nv-he Tsa-ni du-ne-i-s-da-ne-le yv-wi; Ga-do i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se i-na-ge-i?  Wa-so-na-s-go u-no-le u-te-lv-he-s-gv-i?

030725.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎᎢ?  ᎠᏍᎦᏯᏍᎪ ᏗᏬᏍᎩᎵ ᏧᏄᏩᎢ?  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏧᏬᏚᎯ ᏧᎾᏄᏩᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏓᏂᏁᎸ ᎠᏁᎰᎢ.
030725.english: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and live delicately, are in kings' courts.
030725.phonetic: Ga-do-ge i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se-i?  A-s-ga-ya-s-go di-wo-s-gi-li tsu-nu-wa-i?  Gv-ni-yu-quo tsu-wo-du-hi tsu-na-nu-wa-i a-le o-s-dv di-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gi u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi da-ni-ne-lv a-ne-ho-i.

030726.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎨ ᎢᏣᎦᏔᏅᏎᎢ?  ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩᏍᎪ?  ᎥᎥ, ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
030726.english: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.
030726.phonetic: Ga-do-ge i-tsa-ga-ta-nv-se-i?  A-do-le-ho-s-gi-s-go?  V-v, i-tsv-yo-se-ha, a-le u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo a-do-le-ho-s-gi.

030727.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᏅᎵ ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏣᎧᏛ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏣᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎵ ᏣᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ.
030727.english: This is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
030727.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni hi-a a-tsi-no-he-s-gv hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la; Gv-ni-yu-quo tsi-nv-li tsi-nv-si-da-s-di tsa-ka-dv i-gv-yi-di-tlv, na-s-gi da-tsa-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-li tsa-lo-hi-s-di-yi i-gv-yi-di-tlv.

030728.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎭ; ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎬᏩᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏜᏓᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏯᏥᎷᏉᏗ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏣᏂ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏍᏗᎧᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎨᎳ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏣᏂ.
030728.english: For I say unto you, Among those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.
030728.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no ni-tsv-we-se-ha; A-ni-ge-yv gv-wa-ni-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi tsi-gi v-tla yu-dla-s-di gi-lo a-do-le-ho-s-gi u-tli i-ya-tsi-lu-quo-di e-s-ga-quo Tsa-ni Di-da-wo-s-gi; a-se-no u-s-di-ka-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi ge-la u-tli a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu e-s-ga-quo Tsa-ni.

030729.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ, ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᏗᎨᎦᎧᏍᏔᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎨᏎ ᏣᏂ ᏧᏓᏬᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030729.english: And all the people that heard him, and the publicans, justified God, being baptized with the baptism of John.
030729.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no yv-wi u-na-dv-ga-nv-hi, a-le a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi, u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi, di-ge-ga-ka-s-ta-nv-hi-ye-no ge-se Tsa-ni tsu-da-wo-s-do-di ge-sv-i.

030730.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏏᏏᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏚᏓᏅᏖᎮᎸᎢ ᎤᏂᏲᎢᏎᎴ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᏔᏁᎢ, ᏂᏗᎨᎦᏬᎥᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030730.english: But the Pharisees and lawyers rejected the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.
030730.phonetic: A-ni-qua-li-si-s-gi-ni a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-no-si-si-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi du-da-nv-te-he-lv-i u-ni-yo-i-se-le u-nv-sv u-na-da-tlu-ta-ne-i, ni-di-ge-ga-wo-v-na ge-sv-i.

030731.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙᎨᏙᏓᎦᏥᏯᏟᎶᏍᏔᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎪᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ?  ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏓᎾᏤᎸ?
030731.english: And the Lord said, Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?
030731.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do-ge-do-da-ga-tsi-ya-tli-lo-s-ta-ni yv-wi go-hi tsa-ne-ha?  A-le ga-do da-na-tse-lv?

030732.cherokee: ᏗᏂᏲᎵᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎦᏃᏙᏗᏱ ᏣᏂᏃᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏫᏓᎾᏓᏯᏂᏍᎪᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏫᎾᏂᏪᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᏤᎷᎩ ᎣᎬᏔᏅ ᏕᏨᏃᎩᎡᎸ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᎵᏍᎩᏎᎢ; ᏙᏥᏴᎬ ᎢᏣᏛᎪᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏗᏣᏠᏱᎴᎢ.
030732.english: They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.
030732.phonetic: Di-ni-yo-li-quo na-s-gi-ya ga-no-do-di-yi tsa-ni-no-i, a-le tsi-wi-da-na-da-ya-ni-s-go-i, hi-a tsi-wi-na-ni-we-s-go-i; A-tse-lu-gi o-gv-ta-nv de-tsv-no-gi-e-lv, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsa-li-s-gi-se-i; do-tsi-yv-gv i-tsa-dv-go-di, a-se-no v-tla yi-di-tsa-tlo-yi-le-i.

030733.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏰᏃ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎦᏚ ᏯᎩᏍᎨᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᏯᏗᏔᏍᎨᎢ; ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏥᏪᏎ; ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏯᎠ.
030733.english: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil.
030733.phonetic: Tsa-ni-ye-no Di-da-wo-s-gi u-lu-tsv-gi v-tla ga-du ya-gi-s-ge-i, v-tla a-le gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di ya-di-ta-s-ge-i; hi-a-no ni-tsi-we-se; A-s-gi-na u-ya-a.

030734.cherokee: ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏔᏍᎦ; ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏥᏪᎭ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎬᎥᏍᎩ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎠᏗᏔᏍᎩ, ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ.
030734.english: The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners!
030734.phonetic: Yv-wi U-we-tsi u-lu-tsv a-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-ga a-le a-di-ta-s-ga; hi-a-no ni-tsi-we-ha; Gv-ni-yu-quo a-s-ga-ya u-gv-v-s-gi a-li-s-da-yv-di a-le gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di a-di-ta-s-gi, a-de-lv a-ni-gi-si-do-hi a-le a-ni-s-ga-na u-na-li-i.

030735.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᎦᏔᎾᎢ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᎬᏩᏰᎸᎭ
030735.english: But wisdom is justified of all her children.
030735.phonetic: A-se-no a-ga-ta-na-i tsu-we-tsi ni-ga-dv o-si-yu i-gv-wa-ye-lv-ha

030736.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᎾᏁᏗᏱ ᏭᏴᎴᏃ ᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎩᏳᏍᏓᏅᏁ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ.
030736.english: And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee's house, and sat down to meat.
030736.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di A-qua-li-si u-ta-yo-se-le u-li-s-da-yv-na-ne-di-yi Wu-yv-le-no A-qua-li-si ga-ne-lv-i, a-le u-li-gi-yu-s-da-nv-ne a-li-s-da-yv-di-yi.

030737.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᏍᎦᎾᎢ, ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎦᏅᎬ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏲᎴ ᎫᎫ ᏅᏯ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᏟᏍᏕ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ,
030737.english: And, behold, a woman in the city, which was a sinner, when she knew that Jesus sat at meat in the Pharisee's house, brought an alabaster box of ointment,
030737.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-ge-yv na-hna ga-du-hv e-hi, a-s-ga-na-i, u-do-le-ho-sv Tsi-sa A-qua-li-si ga-ne-lv ga-nv-gv a-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i, u-yo-le gu-gu nv-ya u-ne-gv go-tlv-ta-nv-hi a-tli-s-de a-tlo-ne-di,

030738.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏁ ᏚᏜᏓᏌᏛ ᎣᏂᏗᏢ ᏓᏠᏱᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏚᏬᏑᎴᏔᏁ ᏧᎦᏌᏬᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎧᏲᏙᏔᏁ ᎤᏍᏗᏰᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏚᏣᎳᏁ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎶᏁᏔᏁ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ.
030738.english: And stood at his feet behind him weeping, and began to wash his feet with tears, and did wipe them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.
030738.phonetic: A-le u-le-ne du-dla-sa-dv o-ni-di-tlv da-tlo-yi-he-i, a-le u-le-nv-he tsu-la-si-de-ni du-wo-su-le-ta-ne tsu-ga-sa-wo-lv-hi, a-le du-ka-yo-do-ta-ne u-s-di-ye-gv-i, a-le du-du-tsa-la-ne tsu-la-si-de-ni, a-le du-lo-ne-ta-ne a-tlo-ne-di.

030739.cherokee: ᎠᏆᎵᏏᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏭᏯᏅᏛ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᎤᏁᏤ ᏧᏓᏅᏛ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏱᎨᏎ ᏳᏙᎴᎰᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏳᏍᏗᏉ ᎨᏒ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏧᏃᏟᏍᏗᎭ, ᎠᏍᎦᎾᏰᏃ.
030739.english: Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him saw it, he spake within himself, saying, This man, if he were a prophet, would have known who and what manner of woman this is that toucheth him: for she is a sinner.
030739.phonetic: A-qua-li-si-no na-s-gi wu-ya-nv-dv u-go-hv, u-ne-tse tsu-da-nv-dv hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i; Hi-a a-s-ga-ya i-yu-no a-do-le-ho-s-gi yi-ge-se yu-do-le-ho-se na-s-gi ge-sv a-le i-yu-s-di-quo ge-sv hi-a a-ge-yv tsu-no-tli-s-di-ha, a-s-ga-na-ye-no.

030740.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏌᏩᏂ, ᎠᎩᎭ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏲᏎᏗ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᏂᏫᎪᎦ.
030740.english: And Jesus answering said unto him, Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And he saith, Master, say on.
030740.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Sa-wa-ni, a-gi-ha go-hu-s-di gv-yo-se-di.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, ni-wi-go-ga.

030741.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎡᎮ ᏧᏓᏚᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏧᏚᎩ ᎠᏁᎮᎢ; ᏏᏴᏫ ᎯᏍᎩᏧᏈ ᎠᏂᎩᏏ ᏧᎾᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎠᏥᏚᎨᎢ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎯᏍᎦᏍᎪᎯ.
030741.english: There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty.
030741.phonetic: Gi-lo i-yu-s-di e-he tsu-da-du-gi, na-s-gi a-ni-ta-li tsu-du-gi a-ne-he-i; si-yv-wi hi-s-gi-tsu-qui a-ni-gi-si tsu-na-gv-wa-lo-di a-tsi-du-ge-i, so-i-no hi-s-ga-s-go-hi.

030742.cherokee: ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏃ ᏄᏂᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᎫᏴᏙᏗ, ᏚᏲᏎᏉ ᎢᏧᎳ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏍᎩᏃᎲᏏ, ᎦᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏟ ᏄᏓᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ?
030742.english: And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most?
030742.phonetic: Go-hu-s-di-no nu-ni-hv-na ge-sv u-na-gu-yv-do-di, du-yo-se-quo i-tsu-la.  Na-s-gi-no s-gi-no-hv-si, ga-go na-s-gi u-tli nu-da-ge-yu-se-s-di?

030743.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏎᎨᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏗᎬᏩᏲᎯᏎᎸᎯ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏕᎱᎪᏓ.
030743.english: Simon answered and said, I suppose that he, to whom he forgave most. And he said unto him, Thou hast rightly judged.
030743.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni tsu-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-se-ge-li na-s-gi na u-tli i-ga-i di-gv-wa-yo-hi-se-lv-hi.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i; Du-yu-go-dv de-hu-go-da.

030744.cherokee: ᎠᎨᏴᏃ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏅ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏥᏪᏎᎴ ᏌᏩᏂ; ᎯᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎨᏴ?  ᎯᏁᎸ ᎠᎩᏴᎸᎩ, ᎠᎹ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᎩᏁᏁᎴ ᏗᏉᏑᎴᏗ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ; ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᏓᏉᏑᎵᏏ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏧᎦᏌᏬᎸᎯ ᎬᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎩᎧᏲᏙᏓᏏ ᎤᏍᏘᏰᎬᎢ.
030744.english: And he turned to the woman, and said unto Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head.
030744.phonetic: A-ge-yv-no ga-do-gv i-di-tlv wu-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-nv hi-a na-tsi-we-se-le Sa-wa-ni; Hi-go-wa-ti-s-go hi-a a-ge-yv?  Hi-ne-lv a-gi-yv-lv-gi, a-ma v-tla yi-s-gi-ne-ne-le di-quo-su-le-di di-qua-la-si-de-ni; na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na da-quo-su-li-si di-qua-la-si-de-ni tsu-ga-sa-wo-lv-hi gv-da, a-le da-gi-ka-yo-do-da-si u-s-ti-ye-gv-i.

030745.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᏆᏔᏪᏙᏁᎢ; ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎨᏴ, ᎠᎩᏴᎵᎸ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ, ᎥᏝ ᏳᏑᎵᎪᏨ ᏓᏆᏚᏣᎳᏁᎲ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ.
030745.english: Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet.
030745.phonetic: V-tla yi-s-qua-ta-we-do-ne-i; hi-a-s-gi-ni a-ge-yv, a-gi-yv-li-lv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv, v-tla yu-su-li-go-tsv da-qua-du-tsa-la-ne-hv di-qua-la-si-de-ni.

030746.cherokee: ᏥᏍᎪᎵ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏍᎩᎶᏁᏔᏁ ᎪᎢ; ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ ᏚᎶᏁᏔᏅ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏕᏂ.
030746.english: My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.
030746.phonetic: Tsi-s-go-li v-tla yi-s-gi-lo-ne-ta-ne go-i; hi-a-s-gi-ni a-ge-yv a-tlo-ne-di du-lo-ne-ta-nv di-qua-la-si-de-ni.

030747.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏪᏎᎭ; ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏈᏯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᏥᏙᎵᎩ, ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᎤᏓᎨᏳᏎᎢ; ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏙᎵᏨᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᎨᏳᏐᎢ.
030747.english: Wherefore I say unto thee, Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.
030747.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di hi-a ni-gv-we-se-ha; U-s-ga-nv-tsv, na-s-gi tsu-qui-ya tsi-gi, a-tsi-do-li-gi, u-tsa-ti-ye-no u-da-ge-yu-se-i; gi-lo-s-gi-ni u-s-di a-tsi-do-li-tsv-hi, na-s-gi u-s-di u-da-ge-yu-so-i.

030748.english: And he said unto her, Thy sins are forgiven.

030749.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ Ꮎ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎠᏂᏂᏜᏓᎢ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᏙᏧᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ, ᎠᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏕᎦᏙᎵᎦ?
030749.english: And they that sat at meat with him began to say within themselves, Who is this that forgiveth sins also?
030749.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no na i-tsu-la-ha a-ni-ni-dla-i a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv u-na-le-nv-he do-tsu-na-da-nv-dv hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-go na-s-gi hi-a, a-s-ga-nv-tsv na-s-quo tsi-de-ga-do-li-ga?

030750.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ, ᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᏣᏍᏕᎸ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏥᎮᎾ.
030750.english: And he said to the woman, Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.
030750.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le a-ge-yv, Tso-hi-yu-sv tsa-s-de-lv; nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv tsi-he-na.

030801.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᏧᏛᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏙᏁᎢ, ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎮ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎩ, ᏔᎳᏚᏃ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏩᏕᎨᎢ;
030801.english: And it came to pass afterward, that he went throughout every city and village, preaching and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdom of God: and the twelve were with him,
030801.phonetic: O-ni-no i-yv-dv hi-a nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi de-ga-du-wa-di-sv tsu-dv-na a-le tsu-s-di u-we-do-ne-i, a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge a-le gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-he o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv ka-no-he-s-gi, ta-la-du-no i-ya-ni-dv u-na-li-go-wa-de-ge-i;

030802.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏗᎨᏥᏅᏩᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏲ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏩᎾᏕᏯᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏢᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎺᎵ ᎹᎦᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ ᏣᏃᏎᎰᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏄᎪᏤᎸᎯ,
030802.english: And certain women, which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary called Magdalene, out of whom went seven devils,
030802.phonetic: A-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-ge-yv di-ge-tsi-nv-wa-nv-hi u-ni-yo di-da-nv-do gv-wa-na-de-ya-do-di-s-gv a-le du-ni-tlv-gv-i, na-s-gi Me-li Ma-ga-de-li e-hi tsa-no-se-ho-i, na-s-gi ga-li-quo-gi a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-nu-go-tse-lv-hi,

030803.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᏧᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏕᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏑᏌᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎿᎭᎥᎢ.
030803.english: And Joanna the wife of Chuza Herod's steward, and Susanna, and many others, which ministered unto him of their substance.
030803.phonetic: A-le Tso-i-ne u-da-li-i Tsu-sa na-s-gi E-lo-dv u-tse-li tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-ga-sa-ya-s-di-de-gi, a-le Su-sa-ni, a-le a-ni-go-di-yu nv-wa-na-da-le na-s-gi tsi-gv-wa-s-de-lv-do-di-s-ge go-hu-s-di u-ni-hna-v-i.

030804.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳᏃ ᏴᏫ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏦᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏂᏄᎪᏨᎯ, ᎤᏁᏤ ᏚᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ;
030804.english: And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of every city, he spake by a parable:
030804.phonetic: A-ni-go-di-yu-no yv-wi du-ni-la-wi-tso-lv na-s-gi gv-wa-lu-tse-lv de-ga-du-wa-di-sv ni-do-da-yu-ni-nu-go-tsv-hi, u-ne-tse du-tli-lo-s-da-ne-le-i;

030805.cherokee: ᎠᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᏫᏒᏎ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᎦᏔ; ᎤᏫᏒᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏅᏃᎱᎶᏗ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ; ᎤᎾᎳᏍᏓᎡᎴᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏍᏆ ᎦᎳᏅᏛ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎵᏙᎯ ᎤᏂᎪᏁᎢ.
030805.english: A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.
030805.phonetic: A-wi-s-gi u-wi-sv-se u-tse-li u-ga-ta; u-wi-sv-no i-ga-dv nv-no-hu-lo-di u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i; u-na-la-s-da-e-le-no a-le tsi-s-qua ga-la-nv-dv a-ni-no-hi-li-do-hi u-ni-go-ne-i.

030806.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ; ᏧᎵᏰᏅᏉᏃ ᎤᎿᎭᏍᎬᏤ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏄᏬᏕᏫᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
030806.english: And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
030806.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no nv-yo-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i; tsu-li-ye-nv-quo-no u-hna-s-gv-tse nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge nu-wo-de-wi-sv-na ge-sv-i.

030807.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ; ᎠᏄᎦᎸᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏗᎤᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏄᎳᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
030807.english: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.
030807.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no a-nu-ga-lv-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i; a-nu-ga-lv-no i-tsu-la-ha di-u-dv-se-i, a-le u-ne-nu-la-s-ta-ne-i.

030808.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏁᎢ ᎤᏁᏉᏤ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎤᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎩᎶ ᏕᎦᎵᎷᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᏩᏛᎬᎦ
030808.english: And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold. And when he had said these things, he cried, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
030808.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no o-sv ga-do-hi u-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, a-le tsu-dv-se-i, a-le u-ga-ta-ta-ne-i u-ne-quo-tse a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui i-yu-wa-gu-di.  Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-we-sv, u-li-s-ta-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Gi-lo de-ga-li-lu-ge-s-di u-dv-go-di-yi wa-dv-gv-ga

030809.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎬᏩᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎦᏛᎦ ᎯᎠ ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᎢ.
030809.english: And his disciples asked him, saying, What might this parable be?
030809.phonetic: Gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi-no gv-wa-dv-dv-ne hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i; Ga-do ga-dv-ga hi-a da-tli-lo-s-dv-i.

030810.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏂᎯ ᎡᏥᏁᎸᎯ ᎢᏦᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎨᎦᏟᎶᏍᏓᏁᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬ ᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬ ᎬᏩᏃᎵᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
030810.english: And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
030810.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Ni-hi e-tsi-ne-lv-hi i-tso-li-s-di-yi u-de-li-dv ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i; a-ni-so-i-s-gi-ni di-ge-ga-tli-lo-s-da-ne-di; na-s-gi a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-le a-na-dv-gi-s-gv gv-wa-no-li-s-di ni-ge-sv-na.

030811.cherokee: ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗ; ᎤᎦᏔ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᏛᎦ.
030811.english: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
030811.phonetic: Da-tli-lo-s-dv-no hi-a na-s-gi nu-s-di; U-ga-ta ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ga-dv-ga.

030812.cherokee: ᏅᏃᎱᎶᏗᏃ ᏧᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏛᎦ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎦᎷᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏍᎪ ᎦᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏱ, ᎦᎬᏩᏃᎯᏳᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎨᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
030812.english: Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
030812.phonetic: Nv-no-hu-lo-di-no tsu-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i na-s-gi a-na-dv-gi-s-gi ge-tsi-dv-ga; hna-quo-no a-s-gi-na ga-lu-go-i, a-le a-gi-s-go ga-nu-go-wi-s-go ka-no-he-dv tsu-ni-na-wi-yi, ga-gv-wa-no-hi-yu-di a-le ga-ge-tsi-s-de-lv-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

030813.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏧᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎨᏥᏛᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᏥᏓᎾᏓᏂᎸᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏂᎿᎭᏍᏕᏠᎢ; ᏞᎦ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎪᎵᏯ ᎠᏂᏑᎵᎪᎪᎢ.
030813.english: They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.
030813.phonetic: Na-no nv-yo-hi tsu-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i, ge-tsi-dv-ga na-s-gi a-na-dv-gv-ga u-li-he-li-s-di-yu tsi-da-na-da-ni-lv-go ka-no-he-dv; a-le v-tla yi-du-ni-hna-s-de-tlo-i; tle-ga a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-go-i; a-le ge-tsi-go-li-ya a-ni-su-li-go-go-i.

030814.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᎠᏄᎦᎸᎯ ᏧᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ ᎨᏥᏛᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ. ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᏥᏩᏂᎶᏍᎪᎢ, ᎡᎶᎯᏃ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏪᎵᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᏰᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏁᏄᎳᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᎤᎦᏛᎾᏨᎯ.
030814.english: And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.
030814.phonetic: Na-no a-nu-ga-lv-hi tsu-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i ge-tsi-dv-ga na-s-gi a-na-dv-gv-ga. i-lv-hi-tlv tsi-wa-ni-lo-s-go-i, e-lo-hi-no e-hi u-we-li-hi-s-di a-le ge-nv-i-s-di ge-sv, a-le o-s-dv a-ye-lv-di ge-sv a-ni-ne-nu-la-s-di-s-go-i, a-le v-tla ya-ni-na-nu-go-wi-s-go u-ga-dv-na-tsv-hi.

030815.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᎣᏒ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏧᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ ᎨᏥᏛᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏠᎾᏍᏛᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᎾᏫ ᎬᏗ ᎠᎾᏛᎬᎦ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎠᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᏅᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏐᎢ.
030815.english: But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.
030815.phonetic: Na-no o-sv ga-do-hi tsu-la-ge-ya-dv-tse-i ge-tsi-dv-ga na-s-gi nu-tlo-na-s-dv-na a-le o-s-dv ge-sv u-ni-na-wi gv-di a-na-dv-gv-ga ka-no-he-dv, a-ni-s-qua-ni-go-di-s-go-i, a-le a-ni-na-nu-go-wi-s-go u-ga-ta-ta-nv-hi a-nv-ni-di-yu ge-so-i.

030816.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏨᏍᏛᎦ ᎠᏨᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᏱᎫᏢᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏂᏢᏩ ᏱᎦᎧᎲᏍᎪᎢ; ᎦᎪᏗᏱᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎧᎲᏍᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏴᎯᎯ ᎤᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎢᎦᎦᏛᎢ.
030816.english: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.
030816.phonetic: V-tla a-le gi-lo a-tsv-s-dv-ga a-tsv-s-do-di a-te-li-do yi-gu-tlv-di-s-go-i, a-le ga-ni-tlv-wa yi-ga-ka-hv-s-go-i; ga-go-di-yi-s-gi-ni ga-ka-hv-s-go-i, na-s-gi a-ni-yv-hi-hi u-ni-go-wa-dv-di-yi i-ga-ga-dv-i.

030817.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᏍᎦᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
030817.english: For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither any thing hid, that shall not be known and come abroad.
030817.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di u-de-li-dv yi-gi na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na; v-tla a-le go-hu-s-di yi-gv-s-ga-lv na-s-gi a-ga-do-v-hi-s-di a-le gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na.

030818.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎬᎢ, ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎤᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᏄᎲᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎩᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏧᎰ ᏥᏅᏩᏍᏙᎢ.
030818.english: Take heed therefore how ye hear: for whosoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have.
030818.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di nu-s-dv i-tsa-dv-gi-s-gv-i, gi-lo-ye-no u-he-s-di, na-s-gi a-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di; gi-lo-no nu-hv-na ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi a-tsi-gi-e-di ge-se-s-di na-s-gi na tsu-ho tsi-nv-wa-s-do-i.

030819.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴ ᎤᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎾᎥ ᏫᎦᎬᏩᏂᎷᏤᏗ ᏱᎨᏎ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎢ.
030819.english: Then came to him his mother and his brethren, and could not come at him for the press.
030819.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-wa-lu-tse-le u-tsi a-le a-na-li-nv-tli, a-le v-tla na-v wi-ga-gv-wa-ni-lu-tse-di yi-ge-se nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge yv-wi u-ni-tsa-dv-i.

030820.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏃᏁᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᏓᏂᏙᎦ ᎤᎾᏚᎵ ᎨᏣᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ.
030820.english: And it was told him by certain which said, Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to see thee.
030820.phonetic: Gv-wa-no-ne-le-no hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Tsa-tsi a-le i-tsa-li-nv-tli do-yi-di-tlv da-ni-do-ga u-na-du-li ge-tsa-go-wa-dv-di-yi.

030821.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎡᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᎾᏛᎩᎠ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬ ᎢᏯᎾᏛᏁᎯ.
030821.english: And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.
030821.phonetic: U-ne-tsv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; E-tsi a-le o-tsa-li-nv-tli hi-a na-s-gi tsa-na-dv-gi-a ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, a-le na-s-gi ni-ga-we-s-gv i-ya-na-dv-ne-hi.

030822.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎢᏴᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏁ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏎ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ; ᎯᎠᏙ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏗᏗᏐᎩ ᎥᏓᎸ ᏍᎪᎾ ᏫᏗᎶᎯ.  ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏴᏫᏛᎮ.
030822.english: Now it came to pass on a certain day, that he went into a ship with his disciples: and he said unto them, Let us go over unto the other side of the lake. And they launched forth.
030822.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne i-lv-hi-yu i-yv-dv, na-s-gi u-tsa-ne tsi-yu-hi u-na-li-go-se gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi; hi-a-do ni-du-we-se-le-i, Di-di-so-gi v-da-lv s-go-na wi-di-lo-hi.  A-le u-ni-yv-wi-dv-he.

030823.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏂᏒᏃ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎸᏁᎢ; ᎤᏃᎴᏃ ᎤᏱᎶᎴ ᎥᏓᎸᎢ; ᎤᏂᎧᎵᏤᎴᏃ ᎠᎹ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ.
030823.english: But as they sailed he fell asleep: and there came down a storm of wind on the lake; and they were filled with water, and were in jeopardy.
030823.phonetic: A-ni-ni-sv-no tsi-yu u-lv-ne-i; u-no-le-no u-yi-lo-le v-da-lv-i; u-ni-ka-li-tse-le-no a-ma, a-le u-na-ye-hi-s-di nu-na-li-s-da-ne-le-i.

030824.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏰᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᏓᏲᏣᏗᏒᏂ!  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏗᏛᎮ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᎦᏤ ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬ ᎠᎹ; ᎤᏂᏑᎵᎪᏤᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏥᎾᏍᏛᎯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
030824.english: And they came to him, and awoke him, saying, Master, master, we perish. Then he arose, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water: and they ceased, and there was a calm.
030824.phonetic: Gv-wa-lu-tse-le-no a-le gv-wa-ye-s-ta-ne-i, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, Ta-de-yo-hv-s-gi, da-yo-tsa-di-sv-ni!  Hna-quo-no u-di-dv-he a-le du-s-ga-tse ga-no-lv-v-s-gv a-le da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv a-ma; u-ni-su-li-go-tse-no a-le u-da-tsi-na-s-dv-hi nu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

030825.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎭᏝ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏒᎢ?  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ?  ᏕᎧᏁᏤᏰᏃ ᎤᏃᎴ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ.
030825.english: And he said unto them, Where is your faith? And they being afraid wondered, saying one to another, What manner of man is this! for he commandeth even the winds and water, and they obey him.
030825.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ha-tla i-tso-hi-yu-sv-i?  Na-s-gi-no a-ni-s-ga-i-hv u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, hi-a ni-du-na-da-we-se-le-i; Ga-do u-s-di hi-a a-s-ga-ya?  De-ka-ne-tse-ye-no u-no-le a-le a-ma, a-le i-gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga.

030826.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎨᏕᎳᏃ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᏂᎷᏤ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ ᏍᎪᏂᏗᏢ ᏥᎩ.
030826.english: And they arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is over against Galilee.
030826.phonetic: A-ni-ge-de-la-no u-na-tse-li-go-hi wu-ni-lu-tse, na-s-gi Ge-li-li tsu-yu-go-di s-go-ni-di-tlv tsi-gi.

030827.cherokee: ᏭᏣᎢᏒᏃ, ᏚᏠᏎ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏅᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎯ, ᎪᎯᏗᏳ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏯᎢ ᏂᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏓᏄᏬᏍᎨᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ, ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏯᏕᎲᏍᎨᎢ, ᏓᏤᎵᏍᏛᏉᏍᎩᏂ.
030827.english: And when he went forth to land, there met him out of the city a certain man, which had devils long time, and ware no clothes, neither abode in any house, but in the tombs.
030827.phonetic: Wu-tsa-i-sv-no, du-tlo-se gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya ga-du-hv nv-da-yu-nu-go-tsv-hi, go-hi-di-yu a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-ya-i ni-ge-se-i, a-le v-tla yi-da-nu-wo-s-ge-i, v-tla a-le, a-da-ne-lv ya-de-hv-s-ge-i, da-tse-li-s-dv-quo-s-gi-ni.

030828.cherokee: ᎤᎪᎲᏃ ᏥᏌ, ᎤᏪᎷᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏁ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᏗᎩᎾᏓ-ᏛᏙᏗ, ᏥᏌ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏩᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏳ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ?  ᎬᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏍᎩᎩᎸᏅᎩ.
030828.english: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God most high? I beseech thee, torment me not.
030828.phonetic: U-go-hv-no Tsi-sa, u-we-lu-ne-i, a-le u-da-nv-ne ga-do-gv i-gv-yi-di-tlv, a-le a-s-da-ya hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do di-gi-na-da--dv-do-di, Tsi-sa, U-ne-la-nv-hi wa-s-dv ga-lv-la-di-yu e-hi U-we-tsi?  Gv-ta-yo-se-ha tle-s-di s-gi-gi-lv-nv-gi.

030829.cherokee: ᏄᏁᏤᎶᏰᏃ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ.  ᏯᏃᏉᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᏱᏍᎨᎢ; ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎵᏰᏌᎾᎶ ᏓᎦᎸᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ; ᏓᎦᎵᏍᎨᏃ ᏚᏓᎸᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏭᏱᎸᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
030829.english: (For he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For oftentimes it had caught him: and he was kept bound with chains and in fetters; and he brake the bands, and was driven of the devil into the wilderness.)
030829.phonetic: Nu-ne-tse-lo-ye-no ga-da-ha a-da-nv-do u-nu-go-i-s-di-yi a-s-ga-ya.  Ya-no-quo-ye-no u-ni-yi-s-ge-i; tsu-da-de-sv-dv-no a-le di-li-ye-sa-na-lo da-ga-lv-s-di-s-ge-i; da-ga-li-s-ge-no du-da-lv-s-dv-i, a-le a-s-gi-na i-na-ge wu-yi-lv-s-di-s-ge-i.

030830.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᏕᏣᏙᎥ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏅᏗᎤᏪᏎᎢ; ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ; ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏰᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏴᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ.
030830.english: And Jesus asked him, saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: because many devils were entered into him.
030830.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no u-dv-dv-ne hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ga-do de-tsa-do-v?  Hi-a-no nv-di-u-we-se-i; Su-na-da-tlu-gi; u-ni-tsa-te-ye-no a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-yv-lv-hi ge-se-i.

030831.english: And they besought him that he would not command them to go out into the deep.

030832.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏙᎮ ᎦᏚᏏ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏏᏆ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏧᏪᎵᏎᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏚᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᏁᎳᎩᏃ ᏚᏪ ᎵᏎᎴᎢ
030832.english: And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them. And he suffered them.
030832.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-na-li-s-da-yv-ni-do-he ga-du-si u-ni-tsa-ti su-na-da-tlu-gi si-qua; na-s-gi-no gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le u-ne-la-gi tsu-we-li-se-di-yi na-s-gi wi-du-ni-yv-s-di-yi; u-ne-la-gi-no du-we-li-se-le-i

030833.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏄᎪᏤ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᏏᏆ ᏫᎬᏩᏂᏴᎴᎢ; ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩᏃ ᎠᏯᏄᎵᏳ ᎤᎾᎵᏎ ᏭᏂᎦᏐᎠᏎ ᎦᏁᏡᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏓᎸ ᏭᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎬᏤᎢ.
030833.english: Then went the devils out of the man, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the lake, and were choked.
030833.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-nu-go-tse a-s-ga-ya, a-le si-qua wi-gv-wa-ni-yv-le-i; su-na-da-tlu-gi-no a-ya-nu-li-yu u-na-li-se wu-ni-ga-so-a-se ga-ne-tlu-gi ge-sv v-da-lv wu-ni-lu-tse a-le du-ni-gv-tse-i.

030834.cherokee: ᏧᏁᎶᎸᎯᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ ᏚᎾᎵᏘᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏅᏎ ᏭᏂᏃᏁ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᏩᎫᏛᎢ.
030834.english: When they that fed them saw what was done, they fled, and went and told it in the city and in the country.
030834.phonetic: Tsu-ne-lo-lv-hi-no u-ni-go-hv nu-li-s-ta-nv-i du-na-li-ti-se-i, a-le u-ne-nv-se wu-ni-no-ne di-ga-du-hv a-le i-yv-dv wu-wa-gu-dv-i.

030835.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏅᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᎦᏔᏅᏎ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ; ᏥᏌᏃ ᏭᏂᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᎮ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏄᎯᏨᎯ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ, ᎤᏬᎴ ᏥᏌ ᏚᎳᏍᎬᎢ, ᏚᏄᏩᎡᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏌᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ; ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎴᏃ.
030835.english: Then they went out to see what was done; and came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were departed, sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
030835.phonetic: U-ne-nv-se-no u-na-ga-ta-nv-se nu-li-s-ta-nv-i; Tsi-sa-no wu-ni-lu-tse-le-i, a-le u-ni-wa-dv-he a-s-ga-ya, na-s-gi gv-wa-nu-hi-tsv-hi a-ni-s-gi-na, u-wo-le Tsi-sa du-la-s-gv-i, du-nu-wa-e-i, a-le u-da-nv-ti-sa-nv-hi ge-se-i; u-ni-s-ga-le-no.

030836.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏚᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᏄᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏯᎢ ᎠᏥᏅᏩᏅᎢ.
030836.english: They also which saw it told them by what means he that was possessed of the devils was healed.
030836.phonetic: U-ni-go-hv-hi-no na-s-quo du-ni-no-ne-le nu-li-s-do-ta-nv a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-ya-i a-tsi-nv-wa-nv-i.

030837.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᎨᏕᎳ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏗᏁᎯ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᏧᏓᏅᎡᏗᏱ; ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᎢ; ᏥᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏣᏅ ᎢᎤᏨᏎᎢ.
030837.english: Then the whole multitude of the country of the Gadarenes round about besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear: and he went up into the ship, and returned back again.
030837.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ni-ga-di-yu u-ni-tsa-ti A-ni-ge-de-la u-na-tse-li-go-hi ni-gv-na-dv di-ne-hi gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le tsu-da-nv-e-di-yi; u-tsa-ti-ye-no a-ni-s-ga-i-he-i; tsi-yu-hi-no u-tsa-nv i-u-tsv-se-i.

030838.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎬᏩᏄᎪᏤᎸᎯ, ᎤᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏤᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ,
030838.english: Now the man out of whom the devils were departed besought him that he might be with him: but Jesus sent him away, saying,
030838.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-s-ga-ya a-ni-s-gi-na gv-wa-nu-go-tse-lv-hi, u-s-di-ye-ta-ne u-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-di-yi u-s-da-wa-di-da-s-di-yi; a-se-no Tsi-sa u-ne-tse-le u-da-nv-s-di-yi hi-a nu-we-se-le-i,

030839.cherokee: ᏥᎮᎾ ᏗᏤᏅᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏫᏅᎦ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎤᏪᏅᏎᏃ,  ᎠᎴ  ᏭᏃᎮᎵᏙᎴ ᏂᎬᎢ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᏥᏌ.
030839.english: Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee. And he went his way, and published throughout the whole city how great things Jesus had done unto him.
030839.phonetic: Tsi-he-na di-tse-nv-sv-i, a-le gv-ni-ge-sv wi-nv-ga ni-ga-v u-s-qua-ni-go-di ni-tsa-dv-ne-lv U-ne-la-nv-hi.  U-we-nv-se-no,  a-le  wu-no-he-li-do-le ni-gv-i ga-du-hv ni-ga-v u-s-qua-ni-go-di nu-dv-ne-lv Tsi-sa.

030840.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᏥᏌ ᏫᎤᎷᏨ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᏕᎬᏩᏓᏂᎸᏤᎢ; ᏂᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏰᎢ.
030840.english: And it came to pass, that, when Jesus was returned, the people gladly received him: for they were all waiting for him.
030840.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne Tsi-sa wi-u-lu-tsv, na-s-gi yv-wi de-gv-wa-da-ni-lv-tse-i; ni-ga-dv-ye-no gv-wa-ga-ti-ye-i.

030841.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎤᎷᏤᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏤᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎤᏓᏅᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᏴᏍᏗᏱ;
030841.english: And, behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue: and he fell down at Jesus' feet, and besought him that he would come into his house:
